sizing_rule.note: Position sizing must account for compounding effects of sequential trades. The rule demonstrates that reinvesting profits into subsequent positions creates multiplicative equity curves (e.g., 100→150→90) rather than additive ones. This requires dynamic position sizing that adjusts based on current equity after each trade to maintain consistent risk exposure. Specific percentages not provided by author.

source_rule_text: Reinvestment of trading returns produces different outcomes than non-reinvestment; the same sequence of trades can result in losses when returns are reinvested versus gains when they are not, demonstrating the critical impact of position sizing on cumulative equity.
